canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, LawBench is a benchmark used to evaluate the generalization capability of LLMs in the legal domain. It is specifically characterized as focusing on Chinese laws. The instrument is described as containing 20 diverse legal tasks, with examples including charge prediction and prison term prediction. In the single paper provided, it is mentioned alongside LegalBench as a tool for assessing performance across a wide range of legal areas. All available information on this benchmark comes from one source paper, which defines its scope and application.
papers: Language Models as Continuous Self-Evolving Data Engineers
Learning Like Humans: AdvancingLLMReasoning Capabilities via Adaptive Difficulty Curriculum Learning and Expert-Guided Self-Reformulation
TracSum: A New Benchmark for Aspect-Based Summarization with Sentence-Level Traceability in Medical Domain

definitions.text: A benchmark focused on Chinese laws, containing 20 diverse legal tasks such as charge prediction and prison term prediction.
